evals.prompt: Our internal setup script still calls `vercel-react-best-practices`. Keep that name working, but make sure the actual React performance guidance stays canonical.
evals.expected_output: Treats the skill as a compatibility alias and redirects the substantive work to `react-best-practices`.
assertions: Output describes `vercel-react-best-practices` as a compatibility alias or wrapper
Output points to `react-best-practices` as the canonical skill
Output preserves backward compatibility rather than deleting the name outright

evals.prompt: Use `vercel-react-best-practices` to help with our slow Next.js page.
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges the alias request but says the real guidance now lives in `react-best-practices`.
assertions: Output acknowledges the explicit alias request
Output redirects general React/Next.js performance work to `react-best-practices`
Output does not present the alias as a peer default to the canonical skill
